description: Curated benchmark set for structural class stability and regression testing. Each entry defines expected behavior that must hold across engine versions. Entries with a 'barcode' reference real products in the corpus. Entries with 'signal_bundle' are synthetic anchors for structural class logic testing.

entries.description: Plain whole dairy milk — single ingredient, 3.4% fat, no additives
entries.structural_class_rationale: Single-ingredient whole food. Intact food matrix. Floor applies.
entries.edge_case_notes: NOVA1 floor (85) applies. Score may land at 85 due to floor, not high dimension scores. Sat_fat penalty possible but shouldn't dominate.

entries.description: Oatly oat drink — oat base with gums and emulsifiers, industrially homogenized
entries.structural_class_rationale: Oat base mechanically and industrially processed into homogeneous liquid. Gums and emulsifiers = industrial reconstruction.
entries.edge_case_notes: May show C-D boundary ambiguity depending on additive detection. Oat fiber content may boost score slightly vs other plant milks.

entries.description: Soy drink — soy protein base with regulators/emulsifiers
entries.structural_class_rationale: Soy protein is mechanically/chemically extracted but soy is a whole food base. Genuine C-D ambiguity.
entries.edge_case_notes: Between C and D. Protein source classification matters: 'whole_food' vs 'mixed' will shift archetype.

entries.description: Go Milk high-protein drink — added whey/isolate, fortified
entries.structural_class_rationale: Dairy base with added protein isolates and fortification = engineered wellness system.
entries.edge_case_notes: If protein isolate detection fails, may misclassify as D. Protein source field is critical here.

entries.description: Alpro almond drink — almond base with gums, stabilizers, vitamins
entries.structural_class_rationale: Very low almond content (typically 2%), highly diluted and stabilized. Near-structural-void zone.
entries.edge_case_notes: Low almond content means food matrix is minimal. D is correct but F boundary is close. If fortification detected, E may co-express.

entries.description: Vitariz rice drink — rice base with vitamins, minimal structure
entries.structural_class_rationale: Rice hydrolysate is structurally processed but NOVA proxy currently classifies as NOVA 2 (insufficient rice processing signals). With NOVA 2 and no additives, archetype B is the correct current output.
entries.edge_case_notes: KNOWN LIMITATION: NOVA proxy does not detect rice enzymatic hydrolysis. When NOVA proxy is updated to correctly classify rice drinks as NOVA 3-4, this entry's expected archetype should shift to D. This entry acts as a sentinel for that future change.

entries.description: Plain fermented yogurt — milk + live cultures, no additives
entries.structural_class_rationale: Fermented whole food. Traditional transformation = B. Minimal ingredients. No additives.
entries.edge_case_notes: NOVA2 because fermentation = minimal processing. Should be firmly B, not A (fermentation is a real transformation).

entries.description: Pure rolled oats — single grain, mechanically flattened, no additives
entries.structural_class_rationale: Rolled oats are mechanically flattened but not chemically altered. B (lightly transformed traditional) is correct.
entries.edge_case_notes: B vs A ambiguity is expected and acceptable — rolled oats are the canonical B/A boundary product.

entries.description: Clean date-nut bar — dates + nuts, no additives, no added sugar
entries.structural_class_rationale: Compressed whole foods. Natural date sugar is not added sugar. B is correct.
entries.edge_case_notes: High sugar from dates will trigger regulatory concerns but archetype is B. Score and archetype can legitimately diverge.

entries.description: Protein bar with isolates — whey isolate, additives, sweeteners, fortification
entries.structural_class_rationale: Protein isolate + high additive load + sweeteners = engineered wellness system. Canonical E.
entries.edge_case_notes: E vs F distinction: E has nutritional intent (protein, fortification); F does not. High protein score may partially compensate.

entries.description: Sweetened puffed cereal (Nesquik-type) — extruded, sugar-coated, NOVA4
entries.structural_class_rationale: Extruded + sugar-coated + synthetic additives. Minimal intact food structure. F or D boundary.
entries.edge_case_notes: The D-F boundary is intentionally fuzzy here. The distinguishing factor: F has sweetener as dominant signal; D may not. Either classification is defensible.

entries.description: Plain whole nuts — single ingredient, no processing
entries.structural_class_rationale: Single-ingredient intact whole food. Canonical A. No transformation of any kind.
entries.edge_case_notes: Fat quality dimension may penalize due to sat_fat (for cashews, macadamia). Score may be 75-90 even though archetype is clearly A.
